_comment: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for Froglips' path-safety + injection security definitions, consumed by BOTH the Rust backend (include_str! + serde, src-tauri/src/agent/fs.rs, commands/path_safety.rs, agent/shell.rs, agent/injection_scan.rs) and the TS renderer gate (src/lib/agent-loop/dispatch.ts, untrusted-fence.ts). Do NOT re-introduce literal denylist arrays in those files — they filter THIS manifest by flag. A wrong entry here is a vulnerability; parity tests pin every consumer (behavior_snapshot_* in fs.rs/shell.rs, the additive token tests, the cross-language policy fixture).

protectedPaths._doc: Each entry: path = literal absolute path OR path relative to $HOME (homeRelative). read = blocked from the agent read_file/list/search gate. write = blocked from the agent write gate AND the renderer IPC write/read-src gate (path_safety.rs). sandboxDeny = denied (file-read* file-write*) inside the run_shell/run_code Seatbelt cage. Invariant enforced by code: write is ALWAYS true wherever read is true (the write gate calls the read gate first).

absolute._note: /etc + /private/etc stay sandboxDeny:false — builds legitimately read /etc/hosts, resolv.conf, ssl/cert.pem; they are root-owned/world-readable, not secret stores.

homeRelative._carveOut: .aws stays sandboxDeny:false so .aws/config remains shell-readable for aws-cli builds; its credential subpaths (.aws/credentials, .aws/sso) are denied individually below.
.npmrc stays shell-readable so npm/yarn builds resolve registries + scoped auth from the project.
.gitconfig stays shell-readable so git operations in builds pick up user identity / url rewrites.

homeRelative._doc: .aws sub-entries: read/write are already covered by the whole-.aws entry above. They exist as separate rows ONLY so the Seatbelt cage can deny the credential-bearing subpaths (.aws/credentials, .aws/sso) WITHOUT denying .aws/config — which git/npm/aws builds legitimately read (carve-out).

credentialBasenames._doc: Filename-only matches anywhere under home (case-folded). prefixes = startsWith; exact = full case-folded equality. Blocks both read and write.

injectionRoleTokens._doc: Tokenizer-special role-framing sequences that local backends (llama.cpp/MLX) can materialize as REAL role tokens, bypassing the prose 'treat as data' guardrail. UNION of the Rust injection-scan token rows and the TS untrusted-fence strip-list. The Rust scanner derives a regex-escaped literal pattern per token (named via `name`); the context-guarded BOS `<s>` and the literal EOS `</s>` stay Rust-only prose regexes (NOT here — `<s>` would over-strip in TS). The TS fence strips each `token` verbatim.
